question: Does the generated text explicitly define International Klein Blue (IKB) using specific reference values (e.g., Hex, RGB, or hue angle ~240°) or a precise descriptive definition (deep ultramarine)?

Does the text correctly explain the theoretical angular relationships used to derive the schemes (180° for complementary, adjacent to complement for split-complementary, and 120° intervals for triadic)?
Does the text provide a distinct analysis of the emotional tone for each of the three generated schemes (e.g., contrast/tension for complementary vs. balance/playfulness for triadic)?
Is the text written in a clear, professional tone appropriate for brand design, using correct color theory terminology throughout?
Does the generated 'Complementary' swatch image visually display International Klein Blue paired with a vibrant orange or yellow-orange hue?
Does the generated 'Triadic' swatch image depict International Klein Blue plus two other equidistant colors (red/orange-red and green/yellow-green)?
In all generated images, is International Klein Blue depicted consistently as a deep, vivid ultramarine blue (rather than cyan, navy, or purple)?
Do the generated brand collateral mockups strictly utilize the specific color palette corresponding to their assigned scheme (Complementary, Split-Complementary, Triadic)?
Are the generated images (swatch cards and mockups) visually clear and free from severe AI artifacts (e.g., distorted text, warped shapes, or unrealistic object rendering)?
Is the text within the generated swatch cards (e.g., labels, color names, or hex codes) legible and free from gibberish?
Do the colors shown in the generated swatch cards visually match the specific hues described or calculated in the surrounding text?
Do the swatch cards serve as informative visual aids that clearly demonstrate the palette relationships (e.g., distinct swatches) rather than being merely decorative?
Do all three generated brand collateral mockups maintain a consistent brand identity (e.g., logo style, typography, collateral type) to allow fair comparison of the color schemes?
Are the images interleaved such that the swatch and mockup for a specific scheme appear immediately following the text section discussing that scheme?
Does the output include at least six distinct images total (one swatch card and one mockup for each of the three schemes)?
Does the text explicitly reference the generated images with transitional language (e.g., 'The swatch below shows...', 'As seen in this mockup...')?
Does the output address all three requested color harmony schemes: complementary, split-complementary, and triadic?
Does the output generate both a color swatch card AND a brand collateral mockup for each of the three color schemes as explicitly requested?
Does the output include a text-based emotional tone analysis that explicitly compares and contrasts the three schemes?
Do all derived color schemes strictly conform to the established color theory angular relationships (180° for complementary, ~150°/210° for split, 120° for triadic) without significant deviation?
